frameworks.name: Bayesian ideal observer
frameworks.one_line: Trial-by-trial inference over event-timing statistics yielding surprise estimates

frameworks.name: probabilistic inference of environmental statistics
frameworks.one_line: Frames adaptive behavior as tracking latent structure

frameworks.name: neuromodulatory phasic-response account
frameworks.one_line: Brainstem systems broadcast surprise signals to cortex

core_question: Where and when in cortex does surprise about sensory event timing modulate population activity?
key_assumptions: Observers track timing statistics via probabilistic inference
Bayesian ideal-observer model approximates trial-by-trial surprise
Beta-band MEG power indexes cortical population activity
manipulations: Interval timing of changes in a simple visual detection task
Model-derived trial-by-trial temporal surprise versus raw interval
key_findings: Model surprise predicted reaction-time variation better than observed intervals
Surprise correlated negatively with cortical population power
Power suppression was transient around response, specific to beta band
Effect peaked in parietal and prefrontal cortex, distinct from motor beta suppression
interpretations: Temporal surprise drives transient parietal/prefrontal beta suppression
Surprise-related cortical transients are dissociable from motor report signals
